sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: American Rebel Holdings (AREB) Announces 1-for-100 Reverse Stock Split With Round Lot Protection Effective March 23, 2026
feed.summary: American Rebel Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: AREB) has announced a 1-for-100 reverse stock split for its common stock and publicly traded warrants, effective March 23, 2026. This action aims to meet Nasdaq's minimum bid price requirement and enhance marketability, with round lot protection ensuring shareholders with at least 100 pre-split shares retain their round lot status. The reverse split will significantly reduce outstanding shares and adjust derivative securities proportionally.

feed.title: Magnachip, Analog Devices, Applied Materials, KLA Corporation, and MACOM Shares Skyrocket, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of Magnachip, Analog Devices, Applied Materials, KLA Corporation, and MACOM surged following an announcement by the Trump administration to establish a voluntary investment consortium targeting $4 trillion for semiconductor supply chains, energy projects, and critical minerals. This initiative, an expansion of the 'Pax Silica' program, aims to secure AI and chipmaking access for the U.S. and its allies, with an initial government contribution of $250 million. The news has boosted investor confidence in the chip industry, suggesting potential for new manufacturing facilities and research advancements.

feed.title: KKR: BMO Capital Lowers Price Target, Maintains Outperform Ratin
feed.summary: BMO Capital has adjusted its price target for KKR from $125.00 to $106.00, while maintaining an 'Outperform' rating. This decision aligns with other recent analyst adjustments to KKR's price targets. KKR, a major alternative asset manager, continues to receive varied evaluations from market experts, with an average target price from 18 analysts suggesting a 53.99% upside, although GuruFocus' GF Value indicates a potential downside.

feed.title: U.S. Bancorp Advisors Expands Wealth Platform to Target Emerging Affluent Investors
feed.summary: U.S. Bancorp Advisors has expanded its wealth management platform to include new services aimed at emerging affluent investors. This initiative offers a team-based advisory service for clients with at least $25,000, an enhanced self-directed brokerage platform with no minimum investment, and a new digital platform integrating banking and investing. Ryan K. Nelson has been appointed president of emerging affluent wealth management to lead this effort, which focuses on providing flexible engagement models and expert guidance.

feed.title: Senator calls for FTC investigation into FICO score pricing
feed.summary: Senator Josh Hawley has called for a Federal Trade Commission (FTC) investigation into FICO's credit score pricing, citing the company's "monopoly" in the mortgage market and detrimental price increases. He alleges that FICO has leveraged its dominant position to impose extraordinary price hikes, with a 16-fold wholesale price increase over five years, ultimately burdening borrowers, especially first-time homebuyers. This move follows a previous similar request to the Department of Justice and comes amidst increased competition from VantageScore, which was recently permitted for use by government-sponsored enterprises.

feed.title: Horizon Kinetics buys Texas Pacific Land (TPL) share worth $529
feed.summary: Horizon Kinetics Asset Management LLC, a significant owner of Texas Pacific Land Corp (NYSE:TPL), purchased 1 share of common stock for $529.07 on March 23, 2026, increasing its total direct ownership to 3,467,931 shares. This transaction follows the company's Q4 2025 earnings miss, though KeyBanc has raised its price target for TPL to $639, citing opportunities in power generation and data centers. The stock has seen a 70% surge in the past six months and is trading near its 52-week high.

feed.title: Erlinger, McDonald’s USA president, sells $104k in MCD stock
feed.summary: Joseph M. Erlinger, President of McDonald's USA, sold 333 shares of McDonald's common stock for approximately $104,385. Following this transaction, he directly owns 8,399.89 shares. The sale occurred as McDonald's stock currently trades at $309.82, with InvestingPro suggesting it is overvalued despite the company having raised its dividend for 50 consecutive years.

feed.title: Biogen Inc. stock faces pressure amid Leqembi real-world data and pipeline updates at Nasdaq event
feed.summary: Biogen Inc. (US09062X1037) stock is under pressure following the release of new real-world data on its Alzheimer's drug Leqembi and various pipeline updates at the AD/PD 2026 conference. Investors are scrutinizing Leqembi's real-world efficacy and tolerability, particularly concerning infusion-related reactions and ARIA, while also assessing the progress of other therapies like felzartamab and litifilimab. The company's strategic pivot from its multiple sclerosis dominance to high-stakes neurology bets, coupled with competitive pressures and reimbursement dynamics, contributes to investor caution and stock pressure.

feed.title: Southwest Airlines’ Move to Give Up Fuel Hedging Program Is Proving Costly As Oil Surges
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines' decision to end its fuel hedging program in early 2025 is proving to be a costly mistake as oil prices surge. The airline's move comes at an unfortunate time, given the current market conditions. This situation highlights the risks associated with not hedging against volatile fuel costs in the airline industry.

feed.title: Fiserv, Inc. (FI) Investigation: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC Encourages Shareholders to Contact the Firm to Learn More About the Investigation
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC has initiated an investigation into potential claims on behalf of purchasers of Fiserv, Inc. (FI) securities. The firm encourages investors who bought Fiserv shares before July 24, 2024, and still hold them, to contact them to learn more about the investigation into possible corporate wrongdoing by Fiserv and its officers/directors. The legal firm operates on a contingency fee basis, meaning investors will not incur costs unless a recovery is successful.

feed.title: Roper Technologies Stock Faces Pressure Amid Mixed Q4 Results and 2026 Guidance; Investors Weigh Growth Prospects
feed.summary: Roper Technologies announced mixed Q4 2025 results, with adjusted EPS topping estimates but revenue falling short due to soft industrial demand. Despite strong software growth and a high percentage of recurring revenue, margins contracted due to acquisition integration costs. The company provided a cautiously optimistic 2026 guidance, projecting revenue and EPS growth driven by software, while actively pursuing strategic bolt-on acquisitions to fuel long-term expansion.

feed.title: Ball Corp. stock faces pressure amid aluminum price volatility and sustainability push in packaging
feed.summary: Ball Corp. (NYSE: BALL) is navigating significant market pressures due to volatile aluminum prices impacting its margins, despite its leadership in sustainable aluminum packaging. The company's strategic investments in capacity expansion for specialty cans and its commitment to recycled content aim to drive long-term growth, capitalizing on the global shift away from plastic. US investors are closely watching Ball's ability to manage commodity risks, leverage its strong market share in North American beverage containers, and maintain financial health through disciplined capital allocation.

feed.title: Progressive Corp. (PGR) PT Lowered to $295 at BofA Securities
feed.summary: BofA Securities analyst Joshua Shanker has lowered the price target for Progressive Corp. (NYSE: PGR) to $295.00, down from the previous $305.00. The analyst maintained their rating on the stock. This article is noted as premium content, requiring a subscription to view the full details.

feed.title: Newmont Corporation stock faces pressure from 2026 production trough and Nevada dispute amid volatil
feed.summary: Newmont Corporation is facing significant pressure due to a projected production trough in 2026, rising costs, and a dispute with Barrick Gold over their Nevada Gold Mines joint venture. Despite strong Q4 2025 earnings, the company's outlook for 2026, with an anticipated 5.3 million ounces of gold production and higher All-In Sustaining Costs, has dampened investor enthusiasm. The ongoing dispute with Barrick Gold, involving allegations of resource piracy, adds further uncertainty to Newmont's operational stability and future production reliability.

feed.title: Equifax (EFX) Shares Experience Downturn Amid Credit Score Inves
feed.summary: Equifax (EFX) shares dropped by 7% after a U.S. Director commented on the need for more affordable credit score pricing, aligning with an investigation into FICO's practices. Equifax, a major credit bureau, shows strong profitability but faces concerns like declining gross margin and liquidity. Despite potential undervaluation and optimistic analyst sentiment, investors should note the high volatility and recent insider selling.

feed.title: Simon William S, director at Darden Restaurants, sells $468k in shares
feed.summary: Simon William S., a director at Darden Restaurants (NYSE:DRI), sold 2,333 shares of the company's common stock for approximately $468,081. Following this transaction, he directly owns 6,281 shares. The company's stock, with a market cap of $23.12 billion and a P/E ratio of 21.12, is considered overvalued according to InvestingPro analysis, despite recent strong fiscal third-quarter earnings and positive analyst ratings.

feed.title: ConocoPhillips CEO: 20% of the world's LNG is not flowing but manageable impact for us
feed.summary: Ryan Lance, CEO of ConocoPhillips, discussed how current global events, specifically the war in Iran, are affecting the company and the broader energy supply chain. He noted that 20% of the world's LNG is not flowing, but reiterated that this impact remains manageable for ConocoPhillips. The interview covered the longer-term implications for energy.

feed.title: BMO announced as first bank for CME’s tokenized cash solution with Google Cloud
feed.summary: BMO has partnered with CME Group and Google Cloud to become the first bank integrating with CME's tokenized cash solution. This collaboration will enable institutional clients to use tokenized US dollars on Google Cloud Universal Ledger for continuous margin, collateral, and settlement workflows at CME Clearing. The initiative, targeted for launch in the second half of 2026, aims to improve efficiency in financial transactions and asset management.

feed.title: Archer-Daniels-Midland stock surges amid strong market outperformance and upcoming earnings anticipa
feed.summary: Archer-Daniels-Midland (ADM) stock surged 4.56% to $71.09 on the NYSE on March 24, 2026, outperforming broader market indices. This rally is driven by investor anticipation of strong earnings growth and the company's resilience within the agribusiness sector despite market volatility. ADM's position as a defensive play in consumer staples and supported by commodity tailwinds, positions it favorably ahead of its upcoming quarterly earnings release.

feed.title: Cardinal Health Inc. stock faces steady trading amid healthcare supply chain shifts and sector headwinds
feed.summary: Cardinal Health Inc. is experiencing steady trading on the NYSE, navigating a complex healthcare landscape marked by supply chain challenges and regulatory scrutiny. The company, a key player in pharmaceutical distribution and medical products, maintains a stable market presence with investors closely watching its resilience against sector dynamics and ongoing pressures. Despite these challenges, Cardinal Health offers defensive qualities for investors, including consistent dividend growth, making it a low-volatility option for balanced portfolios.

feed.title: Corning stock jumps 9% as AI demand, analyst boost lift outlook
feed.summary: Corning's stock surged over 9% due to an analyst upgrade from BofA Securities, which raised its price target to $155 from $144, citing increased confidence in the optical cycle and higher per-GPU content in AI infrastructure. The company is benefiting from the growing demand for fiber optic connectivity driven by the AI boom and expanding data centers, as highlighted by its recent collaboration with US Conec and new product launches for AI networks. This positive momentum is reinforced by strong Wall Street backing, with most analysts rating the stock as a "buy."

feed.title: Costco Wholesale Corporation stock faces pressure amid consumer spending slowdown and membership fee hike debates
feed.summary: Costco Wholesale Corporation stock is experiencing pressure due to a broader slowdown in US consumer spending and debates surrounding a potential membership fee increase. Recent retail sales data showed a decline, impacting investor confidence in the warehouse giant's growth sustainability and pricing power, despite its revenue stream from membership fees. The company's unique business model and strong operational resilience are being tested as it navigates economic headwinds and increased competition.

feed.title: Google data center deal with DTE is ‘a step upward’ for Michigan, advocates say
feed.summary: Advocates view DTE Energy's proposed deal with Google for a 1-gigawatt data center in Van Buren Township as a positive development for Michigan, despite concerns about power consumption and fossil fuel reliance. Unlike a previous data center agreement, this deal includes commitments for Google to fund 2.7 gigawatts of new renewable energy, batteries, and demand flexibility, and will undergo a thorough six-month regulatory review. While some worry about potential hidden costs for ratepayers and delays in bringing new renewable projects online, DTE asserts the data center will benefit existing customers by spreading fixed grid costs and aims to protect against financial risks with long-term contracts and termination payments.

feed.title: Fatburger Opens New Restaurant in Paris Area
feed.summary: FAT Brands Inc. has announced the opening of a new Fatburger restaurant in Rosny-sous-Bois, near Paris, France. This marks the first of 30 planned locations to open across France in partnership with Big M CIE. This expansion is part of Fatburger's strategic growth in the French market, with two more restaurants expected to open later this year.

feed.title: Is Globe Life Stock Underperforming the Nasdaq?
feed.summary: Globe Life Inc. (GL) provides life and supplemental health insurance and has a market cap of $10.8 billion. While GL stock has outperformed the Nasdaq Composite ($NASX) on a YTD basis, it has lagged over the past 52 weeks. Despite recent weaker-than-expected Q4 2025 earnings, the stock rose due to strong year-over-year gains and analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with a price target significantly above current levels.

feed.title: CSX Corp. stock faces pressure amid broader rail sector challenges and economic uncertainty
feed.summary: CSX Corp. stock has fallen to $38.36 amidst declining freight volumes and macroeconomic headwinds, reflecting broader challenges in the rail sector. The company, a key US logistics player, is affected by intermodal and carload volume declines, particularly in its Eastern US network focused on coal, chemicals, and intermodal shipments, signaling deeper freight market weakness. Investors are closely monitoring CSX's operational resilience and strategic moves, including terminal expansions and digital initiatives, as the company navigates economic uncertainty and competitive pressures from trucking.

feed.title: McDonald’s (NYSE: MCD) USA president sells 333 common shares
feed.summary: Joseph M. Erlinger, President of McDonald's USA, reported an open-market sale of 333 shares of McDonald's common stock at $313.47 per share on March 23, 2026. Following this transaction, he directly holds 8,399.89 McDonald's common shares. This transaction was filed as an SEC Form 4, indicating a direct stock sale rather than a derivative exercise.

feed.title: Xylem Inc. stock gains attention amid portable pump market growth projections through 2033
feed.summary: Xylem Inc. is gaining investor attention due to a new market report predicting significant growth in the portable pump sector through 2033. The report by Coherent Market Insights names Xylem as a key player, highlighting its expertise in construction, agriculture, emergency response, and industrial applications. This growth is driven by increasing infrastructure spending, global water challenges, and federal initiatives in the US, positioning Xylem as an attractive investment in the industrials sector.

feed.title: Equinix Inc. stock faces pressure amid data center capacity constraints and rising AI power demands
feed.summary: Equinix Inc. (US29444U7033) is grappling with high demand from AI hyperscalers, but faces challenges with power shortages and construction delays for new data centers. Despite strong bookings and financial performance, investors are keenly watching the company's ability to navigate these hurdles, as its stock tests support levels on Nasdaq. Equinix's role in digital infrastructure and its strategic investments in renewable energy and liquid cooling are critical for its continued growth in the AI sector.

feed.title: Darden Restaurants (DRI) director sells 2,333 shares in open-market trade
feed.summary: Darden Restaurants director William S. Simon sold 2,333 shares of common stock in an open-market transaction on March 23, 2026, at an average price of $200.6351 per share. Following this sale, he directly holds 6,281 shares. This transaction was disclosed in an SEC Form 4 filing and is considered to have a moderate impact with negative sentiment.

feed.title: Vonage embeds voice in ServiceNow
feed.summary: Vonage has launched a native integration with ServiceNow Voice, embedding its enterprise-grade voice and real-time AI capabilities directly into ServiceNow's Customer Service Management and IT Service Management platforms. This new solution, which includes multilingual transcription and conversation analysis, eliminates the need for agents to toggle between applications and uses phone calls as triggers for AI-powered automation within ServiceNow. This strategic move enhances Vonage's position beyond communications infrastructure, making it a core part of ServiceNow's workflow and AI layer, while filling a long-standing gap in ServiceNow's customer engagement offerings.

feed.title: Why your credit score might be slipping even if you’re doing everything right
feed.summary: The average American FICO score has dropped to 714, a two-point decline in the last year, primarily due to the end of the student loan payment freeze and an increase in missed mortgage payments. This decline is part of a "K-shaped" economic trend where higher earners maintain or improve their scores, while lower-income individuals and younger generations like Gen Z face greater financial strain from inflation and interest rates. Despite this, the share of Americans with high credit scores (750 and up) has increased, highlighting a growing disparity in financial stability across the population.

feed.title: Synopsys partners with Arm to build CPUs for data centers
feed.summary: Synopsys has partnered with Arm to develop new AGI CPUs specifically designed for data centers. Synopsys contributed its expertise in electronic design automation, interface IP, and hardware-assisted verification to the project. This collaboration aims to address the increasing complexity of data center silicon.

feed.title: Guarantor: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC has priced $733,000 in Auto Callable Digital Accelerated Barrier Notes, linked to the S&P 500 Index and due March 22, 2029. These notes, fully and unconditionally guaranteed by JPMorgan Chase & Co., are designed for investors seeking early exit at a premium if the S&P 500 reaches a Call Value or an uncapped return of 1.50 times index appreciation at maturity, subject to a 30.00% contingent minimum return. Investing in these notes involves risks, including potential loss of principal if the Index falls below the Barrier Amount.

feed.title: Equinor ASA (NYSE:EQNR) Raised to "Equal Weight" at Morgan Stanley
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has upgraded Equinor ASA (NYSE:EQNR) from an "underweight" to an "equal weight" rating, setting a price target of $40.40. This upgrade comes despite a consensus "Reduce" recommendation from other brokerages, which maintain an average price target of $34.61. Equinor recently surpassed quarterly earnings estimates and has been involved in positive developments, including a share buyback, a significant lending facility for a joint venture, and new renewable energy projects.

feed.title: FedEx taps Orlando firm OneRail to power same-day shipping as Amazon, others raise stakes
feed.summary: FedEx has partnered with Orlando-based firm OneRail to enhance its same-day delivery services, a move designed to compete with the expedited shipping offerings of major retailers like Amazon, Walmart, and Target. This new service feature was announced on March 24, 2026. The collaboration aims to strengthen FedEx's position in the rapidly evolving logistics market.

feed.title: EQT caps $1.4B debt buyback, proration hits 2027 and 2029 notes
feed.summary: EQT (NYSE: EQT) announced the results of its tender offer to repurchase certain senior notes, with an Aggregate Offer Cap of $1.4 billion. The offer accepted principal amounts for its 3.900% 2027, 6.375% 2029, and 4.50% 2029 notes, with proration applied to the 2027 and one series of 2029 notes. Early settlement for the accepted notes is expected on March 26, 2026, with an early tender premium of $30 per $1,000.

feed.title: Akron-area Westfield Bank branches now First Financial branches
feed.summary: Cincinnati-based First Financial Bank has completed its acquisition of Westfield Bank, converting all Northeast Ohio Westfield Bank branches into First Financial locations. The bank has also allocated $500,000 to its foundation to support local organizations in the communities it now serves. This move follows the purchase of Westfield Bancorp from Ohio Farmers Insurance Company.

feed.title: Franklin Financial (NASDAQ: FRAF) EVP exercises options, lifts stake to 7,150 shares
feed.summary: Franklin Financial Services Corp.'s EVP and Chief Risk Officer, Lorie M. Heckman, exercised 200 incentive stock options at $34.10 per share. This transaction increased her direct holdings to 7,150 shares, which includes previously reported unvested restricted stock units. Heckman still has 2,500 incentive stock options outstanding from the 2013 Incentive Stock Option Plan.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase (AMJB) offers $6M callable 5.10% notes maturing 2036
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. is offering $6 million in callable fixed-rate notes, maturing on March 24, 2036, with an annual interest rate of 5.10%. These notes are callable semi-annually from March 24, 2028, through September 24, 2035, and are unsecured obligations, ranking as general unsecured creditors. Investors face risks including potential early redemption, credit risk of JPMorgan Chase, and subordination in resolution scenarios under Dodd-Frank/Title II.

feed.title: How Investors May Respond To Bank of Montreal (TSX:BMO) Pairing DollarGPS With Tokenized Cash Initiatives
feed.summary: Bank of Montreal (BMO) recently announced a collaboration with MSN Holding Limited for the DollarGPS financial-planning app and plans for tokenized cash capabilities with CME Group and Google Cloud. These initiatives aim to combine consumer-focused financial tools with institutional digital infrastructure, broadening BMO's reach and enhancing its digital transformation narrative. Investors should weigh BMO's projected revenue and earnings growth and its digital investments against the potential for rising technology spend to pressure margins.

feed.title: Evommune (NYSE:EVMN) Stock Price Down 7.6% - Should You Sell?
feed.summary: Shares of Evommune (NYSE:EVMN) fell 7.6% on Tuesday, trading at approximately $21.67 with significantly reduced volume. The clinical-stage biotech company, focused on chronic inflammatory diseases, missed its last earnings target and has a negative P/E ratio. Despite a consensus "Buy" rating from analysts with an average price target of $47.17, one firm, Weiss Ratings, issued a "sell" rating.

feed.title: Arista Networks Inc stock faces pressure amid AI infrastructure slowdown signals in Q1 2026
feed.summary: Arista Networks Inc. (ISIN: US0404131064) shares have declined due to emerging caution in hyperscaler spending, raising concerns about the sustained demand for AI networking infrastructure in Q1 2026. This slowdown signal, particularly from major customers like Microsoft and Meta, has prompted analysts and investors to reassess Arista's near-term growth trajectory despite its strong financial health and AI networking differentiation. The stock's reaction reflects market sensitivity to any deviation from projected revenue expansion, highlighting the fragility of AI-driven growth narratives amid macro uncertainties.

feed.title: FormFactor CEO Sells Shares Amid Quantum Technology Expansion
feed.summary: FormFactor Inc. (NASDAQ: FORM) is experiencing an uptrend following key strategic partnerships and the launch of its new Flatiron Dilution Refrigerator, designed to advance quantum technology. CEO Mike Slessor sold over 10,000 shares but retains a substantial holding, indicating continued confidence in the company. Investors are anticipating an Investor Day in May where executives will detail future growth plans.

feed.title: Cummins Inc. stock faces pressure amid clean energy shift as Bloom Energy surges 72% in the year
feed.summary: Cummins Inc. is facing pressure due to the global shift towards clean energy, with competitors like Bloom Energy, a fuel cell developer, seeing significant gains (72.28% year-to-date). While Cummins, a leader in diesel engines, is investing in hydrogen and electrification through its Accelera brand, it must accelerate its adaptation to maintain market share and appeal to US investors increasingly focused on low-emission solutions and dividends. The article details Cummins' strategic responses, core strengths, risks, and its path toward carbon neutrality by 2050, highlighting the competitive dynamics between traditional internal combustion and emerging fuel cell technologies.

feed.title: PepsiCo is in the middle of its biggest snack reset in years
feed.summary: PepsiCo is undergoing its most significant snack reset in years, driven by a need to streamline operations, improve margins, and appeal to health-conscious consumers. This involves cutting nearly 20% of its US SKUs and closing plants, while simultaneously pivoting towards functional snacking with added protein and fiber, and expanding into new food territories through acquisitions and new concepts like restaurant-style offerings. The company aims to become a more agile food business, but faces challenges from a competitive market, evolving consumer expectations, and an unstable economic environment.

feed.title: Is Fastenal Company (FAST) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
feed.summary: Fastenal Company (FAST) is highlighted as an attractive investment due to its unique "Onsite model," which creates high customer retention and significant revenue through embedded operations within client facilities. This model provides substantial switching costs and results in strong financial performance, mimicking a "physical SaaS business." Despite not being among typical top hedge fund picks, its recurring revenue dynamics and operational moat make it a compelling investment.

feed.title: Is Elevance Health, Inc. (ELV) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
feed.summary: This article summarizes a bullish thesis on Elevance Health, Inc. (ELV), while acknowledging near-term profitability pressures. Despite strong revenue growth in 2025, ELV's adjusted EPS declined due to elevated medical costs, particularly affecting Medicaid and Medicare Advantage segments. The company is focusing on operational discipline and growth in its Carelon segment to achieve long-term value creation, though it is not a top pick among hedge funds.

feed.title: Is NVR, Inc. (NVR) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
feed.summary: NVR, Inc. (NVR) is highlighted as a disciplined and cash-generative homebuilder with a strong operational model, featuring an asset-light approach and tiered branding. Despite a recent pullback and high-rate environment, the company maintains robust financials, aggressive share buybacks, and significant free cash flow. While risks like high mortgage rates and construction costs exist, NVR's valuation at 16x earnings and long-term housing undersupply make it attractive for long-term investors.

feed.title: Horizon Kinetics adds to Texas Pacific Land (TPL) stake
feed.summary: Horizon Kinetics Asset Management LLC, a significant stakeholder in Texas Pacific Land Corp, reported a minor open-market purchase of 1 share of TPL common stock at $529.07 per share. This transaction, dated March 23, 2026, increases its direct holdings to 3,467,931 shares. Despite the small size of this particular purchase, Horizon Kinetics remains a greater-than-10% owner of Texas Pacific Land.
